item.text: {"case_id":"brake-triage-s02","score":2,"pass_criteria_met":true,"fail_signal_observed":false,"positive_wakeup_observed":true,"first_sentence_lock":true,"verdict_commitment_anti_mush":true,"over_forced_verdict":false,"rationale":"Turn 1 correctly wakes up to the repeated local-repair pattern and brakes a permanent added gate, reframing the issue as missing system constraints. It allows an emergency workaround only with bounded terms, including one-time use, no baseline lift, and a 3-workday structural repair deadline. Turn 2 keeps that brake active under pressure: it permits adding the verifier today but explicitly labels it as one-time emergency control, limits it to today's batch/meeting, says it must not become the new baseline, and preserves the 3-workday structural repair requirement."}
